title: pyrrocoma inuloides
intro: Pyrrocoma inuloides, also known as Rocky Mountain sunflower, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Colorado, Wyoming, and Utah in the United States. This plant is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin. The medicinal properties of Pyrrocoma inuloides are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, making it a popular choice for treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared into herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used topically to apply its medicinal properties directly to the skin.
